sources.WDI: World Bank World Development Indicators, https://data.worldbank.org
sources.BBS: Bangladesh Bureau of Statistics, Labour Force Survey 2022

data_gaps: Informal sector wage data: BBS LFS 2022 covers formal wages; informal sector poorly tracked
Occupation-level wage data: Not available at ISCO-08 disaggregation
Garment sector micro-data: Brand audits have better wage data than government surveys
Migration remittance impact on labour supply not captured in standard LFS

gdp_per_capita_usd.note: Steady growth trajectory; tripled from $838 in 2010

youth_unemployment_rate_pct.note: Significant underemployment and disguised unemployment not captured

youth_neet_pct.note: Heavily influenced by low female labour force participation

female_labour_participation_pct.note: One of the lowest in the region; garment sector is primary female employer

wages._note: Formal sector data. RMG minimum wage revised to 12,500 BDT/month (~$113) in Nov 2023.

human_capital_index.note: Below South Asian average of 0.48; improving rapidly

mobile_money_accounts_pct.note: bKash and Nagad dominate; 30M+ active MFS accounts

ready_made_garments.note: Diversifying beyond basic garments to value-added textiles; automation pressure rising

it_bpo.note: Government Hi-Tech Park initiative; $1.4B+ IT exports in 2023

agriculture_processing.note: Cold chain and agritech investment accelerating

financial_services.note: MFS and fintech ecosystem (bKash, Nagad, ShopUp)

construction.note: Megaprojects: Padma Bridge, metro rail, economic zones

remittance_services.note: $21B+ annual remittances; shift to digital channels
